name: Triphala
traditionalIndication: Traditionally used for bowel regularity, digestive support, and rasayana contexts.
anupana: Warm water is commonly referenced; individualized guidance is preferred.
cautions: Caution with dehydration, diarrhea, pregnancy, and medicines

name: Trikatu
traditionalIndication: Traditionally used for manda agni and kapha-associated heaviness.
anupana: Often honey or warm water in traditional contexts; practitioner guided.
cautions: Can aggravate burning, acidity, ulcers, or strong pitta signs

name: Hingvastak
traditionalIndication: Traditionally used for vata-type gas, bloating, and sluggish digestion.
anupana: Traditionally taken with warm food or ghee depending on context; practitioner guided.

name: Sitopaladi
traditionalIndication: Traditionally used for cough, throat comfort, and mild kapha-pitta respiratory patterns.
anupana: Honey is commonly referenced; avoid honey for infants and use practitioner guidance.
cautions: Sugar content may be unsuitable for diabetes or glucose control issues

name: Talisadi
traditionalIndication: Traditionally used for kapha-associated cough with digestive sluggishness.
anupana: Honey or warm water in traditional contexts; practitioner guided.
cautions: Heating spices and sugar may not suit acidity, diabetes, or sensitive stomach

name: Chyawanprash
traditionalIndication: Traditionally used as rasayana and respiratory-strength support.
anupana: Usually taken as avalehya; context and product composition matter.
cautions: Complex ingredients require allergy and medicine review

name: Dashmool
traditionalIndication: Traditionally used for vata-kapha patterns, joint support, and respiratory contexts.
anupana: Often decoction or formulation-specific vehicle; practitioner guided.
cautions: Botanical identity and formulation quality matter
contraindications: Serious chronic disease without clinician review

name: Kanchanar Guggulu
traditionalIndication: Traditionally used in kapha-related glandular swelling contexts under practitioner care.
anupana: Varies by clinical context; qualified supervision required.
cautions: Possible interactions with thyroid medicines, anticoagulants, and chronic disease medicines

name: Punarnava Mandur
traditionalIndication: Traditionally used in anemia-related and fluid-balance contexts under qualified supervision.
cautions: Requires diagnosis, quality assurance, and monitoring
contraindications: Liver or kidney disease without clinician review

name: Avipattikar
traditionalIndication: Traditionally used for pitta-associated acidity and digestive discomfort under guidance.
anupana: Often warm water; individualized guidance is preferred.
cautions: Sugar content may matter for diabetes
